prediction.reasoning: Based on typical daily volatility for large-cap consumer staples stocks like Procter & Gamble, I estimate a daily standard deviation of approximately 1.3%. PG tends to have lower volatility due to its defensive nature and stable dividend profile. The point estimate assumes minimal drift with slight positive bias typical of established dividend aristocrats. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions around the estimated daily volatility.
